body.title: Consequence check: advisor escalation in sprint-ticket
body.result: Observation: advisor escalation points just shipped in this session — no malfunctions observed; both step 2.5 (reset_count check) and step 8 (3rd failure) correctly wired. Consequence check: clear
body.intention: I intend to verify that the advisor escalation points reduce stalled-sprint token waste without introducing friction on clean sprints.
body.description:
**What changed:** sprint-ticket now calls advisor() when reset_count > 0 and after 3 test failures.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Advisor call on clean sprints: if reset_count check misfires (e.g. ticket was reset for reasons unrelated to failure), advisor is called unnecessarily — adds latency but no harm
- Advisor adds context cost: each advisor() call consumes context; in a long sprint this may push toward compaction earlier than expected
- 3-failure heuristic ambiguity: 'same test failing' requires the model to track failure history — may be applied inconsistently

**Signals to watch:** Advisor called in sprint sessions where reset_count > 0 (Granny log shows prior reset); sprint sessions that previously looped now escalate and either converge or hold cleanly.

**Gate condition:** Check after 5 autonomous sprints complete with reset_count > 0 tickets.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe whether advisor is called at the right moments and whether it improves convergence
**Completion criteria:** no automated verification — observation ticket; done when effects annotated as occurred / clear / partial and closed
